+{"id":"bd-39rni","title":"test(runtime): fix undo/transaction.rs helpers + assertions for execute vs add_executed","description":"In crates/ftui-runtime/src/undo/transaction.rs tests, TransactionScope::execute / Transaction::execute must receive *unexecuted* commands (they execute internally), while add_executed expects a pre-executed command. Harden the tests by splitting helpers (make_cmd pre-executed vs make_scope_cmd unexecuted), using safe truncate-based remove logic, and adding buffer-state assertions to catch double-execution / rollback bugs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:50:19.328374671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:50:19.328374671Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-39vx","title":"Modal/Dialog System with Proper Stacking","description":"## Background

FrankenTUI needs modals for:
- Confirmations ('Delete file?')
- Prompts ('Enter filename:')
- Complex dialogs (settings, wizards)
- Help overlay (already exists, but ad-hoc)
- Command palette (planned in bd-39y4)

Current approach: Each feature implements its own overlay logic.

## Solution: Modal Layer System

```rust
/// Modal size presets
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum ModalSize {
    Small,      // 40x10
    Medium,     // 60x20
    Large,      // 80%x80%
    FullScreen,
    Custom { width: Constraint, height: Constraint },
}

/// A modal dialog
pub struct Modal {
    pub id: ModalId,
    pub content: Box<dyn Widget>,
    pub size: ModalSize,
    pub title: Option<String>,
    pub dismissable: bool,  // Escape closes
    pub backdrop: BackdropStyle,
    pub animation: Option<ModalAnimation>,
    pub on_dismiss: Option<Box<dyn FnOnce() + Send>>,
}

/// Backdrop behind modal
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum BackdropStyle {
    None,
    Dim { opacity: f32 },  // 0.0-1.0
    Blur,  // If terminal supports
    Custom(Style),
}

/// Modal animation
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum ModalAnimation {
    FadeIn { duration: Duration },
    SlideUp { duration: Duration },
    Scale { duration: Duration },
}

/// Manages modal stack
pub struct ModalLayer {
    stack: Vec<Modal>,
    focus_manager: FocusManager,  // Uses #2
}

impl ModalLayer {
    pub fn push(&mut self, modal: Modal) -> ModalId;
    pub fn pop(&mut self) -> Option<Modal>;
    pub fn close(&mut self, id: ModalId);
    pub fn is_open(&self, id: ModalId) -> bool;
    pub fn has_modals(&self) -> bool;
    pub fn render(&self, frame: &mut Frame); // Renders all modals
}

// Convenience builders
pub fn confirm(title: &str, message: &str) -> ConfirmDialogBuilder;
pub fn prompt(title: &str, placeholder: &str) -> PromptDialogBuilder;
pub fn alert(title: &str, message: &str) -> AlertDialogBuilder;

// Usage
let result = confirm("Delete?", "This cannot be undone")
    .with_cancel("Cancel")
    .with_confirm("Delete")
    .danger()  // Red confirm button
    .show(&mut modal_layer);
```

## Visual Design

```
┌──────────── Delete File? ────────────┐
│                                       │
│  Are you sure you want to delete     │
│  'important.txt'?                    │
│                                       │
│  This action cannot be undone.       │
│                                       │
│           [Cancel]  [Delete]          │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Focus Trapping (uses #2)

- Modal pushes focus trap on open
- Tab cycles within modal
- Escape pops modal and restores focus

## Files to Create

- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/mod.rs
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/confirm.rs
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/prompt.rs
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/alert.rs
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/modal_layer.rs

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Modal/Dialog System with Proper Stacking
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests
- Formal invariants documented + enforced via property tests; any dynamic decisions have an evidence ledger and debug UI


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
- Metamorphic tests: shrink/grow, reorder non-semantic inputs, and ensure invariants still hold.

 {"id":"bd-39vx.1","title":"Modal Container Widget","description":"Core modal widget that renders content in overlay layer.

## Requirements
- Centered positioning with configurable offset
- Backdrop with configurable opacity
- Size constraints (min/max width/height)
- Close on backdrop click (optional)

## Proposed API

```rust
pub struct Modal<C: Widget> {
    content: C,
    config: ModalConfig,
    state: ModalState,
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct ModalConfig {
    pub position: ModalPosition,
    pub backdrop: BackdropConfig,
    pub size: SizeConstraints,
    pub close_on_backdrop: bool,
    pub close_on_escape: bool,
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub enum ModalPosition {
    Center,
    CenterOffset { x: i16, y: i16 },
    TopCenter { margin: u16 },
    Custom { x: u16, y: u16 },
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct BackdropConfig {
    pub color: Color,
    pub opacity: f32,  // 0.0 - 1.0
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct SizeConstraints {
    pub min_width: Option<u16>,
    pub max_width: Option<u16>,
    pub min_height: Option<u16>,
    pub max_height: Option<u16>,
}

pub enum ModalMsg {
    Close,
    BackdropClicked,
    EscapePressed,
}
```

## Rendering Strategy
1. Render backdrop to full screen area
2. Calculate content bounds from constraints
3. Center content within available space
4. Render content on top of backdrop
5. Use overlay layer (z-index management)

## File: crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/container.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_center_calculation: Various screen sizes
- test_offset_positioning: Offset from center
- test_size_constraints: Min/max enforcement
- test_backdrop_click: Click detection on backdrop vs content

## Snapshot Tests
- modal_center_80x24, modal_offset_80x24
- modal_constrained_120x40, modal_backdrop_opacity

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Modal Container Widget
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-39vx.2","title":"Dialog Presets","description":"Common dialog patterns built on Modal.

## Requirements
- Alert dialog (message + OK button)
- Confirm dialog (message + OK/Cancel)
- Prompt dialog (message + input + OK/Cancel)
- Custom dialog builder

## Implementation
- Dialog enum with Alert, Confirm, Prompt variants
- DialogBuilder for custom dialogs
- Async result handling via Cmd

## Tests
- Each dialog type renders correctly
- Button actions work
- Input capture in prompt

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Dialog Presets
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

- Markdown renderer currently shows raw Mermaid code fences.

Scope
- Detect ```mermaid fences in MarkdownRenderer.
- Parse Mermaid content and render via TME pipeline.
- Apply init directives and config overrides where allowed.
- Apply link policy and render hyperlinks/footnotes when enabled.
- On parse/layout errors, render structured error panel (with location) and optional raw snippet.
- Feature flag to enable/disable Mermaid rendering.
- Log render outcomes to JSONL (success/error, size, tier, warnings).

Considerations
- Rendering must respect available width/height for the panel.
- Errors must not crash or poison caches.

Tests / Validation
- Snapshot tests for Mermaid rendering in Markdown screen.
- Error-panel snapshot tests for malformed Mermaid.

Success Criteria
- Mermaid fences display diagrams, not raw code.
- Errors render as readable panels with location  {"id":"bd-3nohm","title":"Fix lexer byte-to-char casts for non-ASCII UTF-8 support","description":"The Mermaid lexer uses 'b as char' casts at 6 locations which incorrectly handle non-ASCII bytes. Multi-byte UTF-8 characters (CJK node names, emoji) get split into separate invalid Punct tokens. Fix: decode full UTF-8 chars and handle non-ASCII in identifier lexing.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:14:42.986648885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:26:03.548236676Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:19:12.269990645Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":788,"issue_id":"bd-3nohm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear claiming. Will fix the 6 byte-to-char cast locations in the Mermaid lexer to properly handle non-ASCII UTF-8 characters.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:18:00Z"},{"id":789,"issue_id":"bd-3nohm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed lexer byte-to-char casts: added decode_current_char()/advance_char() for UTF-8, updated is_ident_start/is_ident_continue for Unicode letters, fixed next_token/lex_identifier/lex_arrow_or_punct to branch on ASCII vs non-ASCII. Added tokenize_non_ascii_identifiers test. All 402 mermaid tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:19:08Z"},{"id":790,"issue_id":"bd-3nohm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear: Fixed. The core bug (non-ASCII bytes split into separate Punct tokens) was already fixed by a previous agent. I additionally:
1. Fixed column tracking in lex_string() and lex_comment_or_directive() — non-ASCII chars inside strings/comments now advance by 1 column instead of N bytes, via new advance_byte_or_char() helper
2. Added 6 comprehensive non-ASCII tests: CJK identifiers, CJK strings, emoji strings, emoji as Punct, CJK comments, and span column counting verification
3. Fixed pre-existing compilation errors: added missing 'constraints' field to MermaidDiagramIr constructors in mermaid.rs, dot_parser.rs, and diagram_layout_bench.rs
All 486 ftui-extras tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:26:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3o1l","title":"Text Effects: Mirror / Reflection","description":"## Background
Mirror/reflection creates a classic 'glossy' text effect with a vertically flipped copy below. Works especially well for titles using AsciiArtText, terminal-appropriate since it uses vertical space.

## Implementation

Add Reflection struct (separate from TextEffect for flexibility):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Reflection {
    pub gap: u16,            // Rows between text and reflection (0-3 typical)
    pub opacity_start: f64,  // Top of reflection (0.3-0.5 typical)
    pub opacity_end: f64,    // Bottom of reflection (0.0 typical)
    pub height_ratio: f64,   // 0.0-1.0, how much of text height to reflect
    pub wave: f64,           // 0.0=perfect mirror, 1.0=wavy water effect
}

impl Default for Reflection {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            gap: 1,
            opacity_start: 0.4,
            opacity_end: 0.0,
            height_ratio: 0.5,  // Reflect bottom half of text
            wave: 0.0,
        }
    }
}
```

### Usage with StyledText:
```rust
// Only works with multi-line text (AsciiArtText or actual multi-line)
let art = AsciiArtText::new("TITLE", AsciiArtStyle::Block);
StyledText::from_ascii_art(art)
    .reflection(Reflection::default())
    .effect(TextEffect::HorizontalGradient { ... })
```

### Single-Line Behavior:
For single-line text, reflection is a no-op (nothing to flip vertically). Use AsciiArtText for reflections on titles.

### Rendering:
```rust
fn render_with_reflection(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
    // 1. Render main text
    self.render_text(main_area, frame);
    
    // 2. Calculate reflection area
    let reflect_height = (self.text_height as f64 * self.reflection.height_ratio) as u16;
    let reflect_y = main_area.bottom() + self.reflection.gap;
    
    // 3. Render flipped text with gradient opacity
    for row in 0..reflect_height {
        let source_row = self.text_height - 1 - row;  // Flip vertically
        let opacity = lerp(self.reflection.opacity_start, 
                          self.reflection.opacity_end,
                          row as f64 / reflect_height as f64);
        
        let wave_offset = if self.reflection.wave > 0.0 {
            (row as f64 * 0.5 + self.time * 2.0).sin() * self.reflection.wave
        } else { 0.0 };
        
        self.render_row_with_opacity(source_row, reflect_y + row, opacity, wave_offset, frame);
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-rk95","title":"Implement GraphemeId encoding with slot+width packing","description":"# Implement GraphemeId encoding with slot+width packing

## Background
Plan Section 5.1 defines the specific encoding for GraphemeId - the reference to complex grapheme clusters in the GraphemePool. This encoding is critical for the 16-byte Cell constraint.

## Encoding Specification
```
GraphemeId: u32
Layout: [30-24: width (7 bits)][23-0: pool slot (24 bits)]
```

### Bit Layout
```
MSB                                      LSB
+-------+----------------------------------+
| Width | Pool Slot Index                  |
| 7 bits| 24 bits                          |
+-------+----------------------------------+
Bits: 30-24  23-0
```

### Capacity
- Pool slots: 16,777,216 (24 bits = 16M entries)
- Width range: 0-127 (7 bits, plenty for any display width)

## Implementation

```rust
/// Grapheme ID: reference to interned string in GraphemePool
/// Layout: [30-24: width (7 bits)][23-0: pool slot (24 bits)]
/// 
/// # Design Rationale
/// - 24 bits for slot allows 16M unique graphemes (far exceeding practical usage)
/// - 7 bits for width allows display widths 0-127 (most graphemes are 1-2)
/// - Embedded width avoids pool lookup for width queries
/// - Total 31 bits leaves bit 31 for CellContent type discrimination
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
#[repr(transparent)]
pub struct GraphemeId(u32);

impl GraphemeId {
    /// Maximum slot index (24 bits)
    pub const MAX_SLOT: u32 = 0x00FF_FFFF;
    
    /// Maximum width (7 bits)
    pub const MAX_WIDTH: u8 = 127;
    
    /// Create from slot index and display width
    /// 
    /// # Panics
    /// Panics in debug mode if slot > MAX_SLOT or width > MAX_WIDTH
    #[inline]
    pub fn new(slot: u32, width: u8) -> Self {
        debug_assert!(slot <= Self::MAX_SLOT, "slot overflow: {}", slot);
        debug_assert!(width <= Self::MAX_WIDTH, "width overflow: {}", width);
        Self((slot & Self::MAX_SLOT) | ((width as u32) << 24))
    }
    
    /// Extract pool slot index (0-16M)
    #[inline]
    pub fn slot(&self) -> usize {
        (self.0 & Self::MAX_SLOT) as usize
    }
    
    /// Extract display width (0-127)
    #[inline]
    pub fn width(&self) -> usize {
        ((self.0 >> 24) & 0x7F) as usize
    }
    
    /// Raw u32 value for storage in CellContent
    #[inline]
    pub fn raw(&self) -> u32 {
        self.0
    }
    
    /// Reconstruct from raw u32
    #[inline]
    pub fn from_raw(raw: u32) -> Self {
        Self(raw)
    }
}

impl std::fmt::Debug for GraphemeId {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        f.debug_struct("GraphemeId")
            .field("slot", &self.slot())
            .field("width", &self.width())
            .finish()
    }
}
```

## Integration with CellContent

CellContent uses bit 31 to discriminate between char and GraphemeId:
- Bit 31 == 0: char (Unicode scalar value in bits 0-30)
- Bit 31 == 1: GraphemeId (slot + width in bits 0-30)

```rust
impl CellContent {
    pub fn from_grapheme(id: GraphemeId) -> Self {
        // Set bit 31 to indicate grapheme
        Self(0x8000_0000 | id.raw())
    }
    
    pub fn grapheme_id(&self) -> Option<GraphemeId> {
        if self.is_grapheme() {
            Some(GraphemeId::from_raw(self.0 & !0x8000_0000))
        } else {
            None
        }
    }
}
```

## Tests Required

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    #[test]
    fn test_grapheme_id_encoding() {
        // Test basic encoding
        let id = GraphemeId::new(12345, 2);
        assert_eq!(id.slot(), 12345);
        assert_eq!(id.width(), 2);
        
        // Test max values
        let id_max = GraphemeId::new(GraphemeId::MAX_SLOT, GraphemeId::MAX_WIDTH);
        assert_eq!(id_max.slot(), 0x00FF_FFFF);
        assert_eq!(id_max.width(), 127);
        
        // Test zero values
        let id_zero = GraphemeId::new(0, 0);
        assert_eq!(id_zero.slot(), 0);
        assert_eq!(id_zero.width(), 0);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_grapheme_id_roundtrip() {
        for slot in [0, 1, 100, 10000, 0x00FF_FFFF] {
            for width in [0, 1, 2, 4, 127] {
                let id = GraphemeId::new(slot, width);
                assert_eq!(id.slot() as u32, slot);
                assert_eq!(id.width() as u8, width);
            }
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_cellcontent_grapheme_integration() {
        let id = GraphemeId::new(42, 2);
        let content = CellContent::from_grapheme(id);
        
        assert!(content.is_grapheme());
        assert_eq!(content.grapheme_id(), Some(id));
        assert_eq!(content.width(), 2);
    }
    
    #[test]
    #[should_panic(expected = "slot overflow")]
    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]
    fn test_grapheme_id_slot_overflow_debug() {
        let _ = GraphemeId::new(0x0100_0000, 1); // 25 bits
    }
    
    #[test]
    #[should_panic(expected = "width overflow")]
    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]
    fn test_grapheme_id_width_overflow_debug() {
        let _ = GraphemeId::new(1, 128);
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GraphemeId fits in u32 with documented layout
- [ ] slot() and width() extract correctly
- [ ] Integration with CellContent works
- [ ] Debug assertions catch overflows
- [ ] Tests cover edge cases

 {"id":"bd-tb84","title":"v1 Definition of Done (Ship Checklist)","description":"# v1 Definition of Done (Ship Checklist)

## Background
Plan Section 0.11 defines when ftui "v1" is done. This is the comprehensive checklist that must be completed before tagging v1.0.0.

## Definition of Done Criteria

### 1. Inline Mode Default is Stable
- [ ] Streaming logs + UI chrome does not corrupt scrollback
- [ ] Streaming logs + UI chrome does not corrupt cursor placement
- [ ] PTY tests confirm behavior on normal exit
- [ ] PTY tests confirm behavior on panic
- [ ] Tested on: Linux (xterm-like), macOS Terminal, tmux, screen
- [ ] No flicker under sustained log output
- [ ] Resize during logging re-anchors correctly

**Verified by:** bd-2gx Gate 1, bd-fbp, bd-10i.11.2

### 2. Diff/Presenter Correctness Validated
- [ ] Terminal-model tests for supported sequences pass
- [ ] Property tests with random buffers pass
- [ ] Output size baselines for representative workloads documented
- [ ] Style state never leaks across runs
- [ ] OSC 8 links properly managed (open/close)

**Verified by:** bd-2gx Gate 2, bd-10i.11.1, bd-10i.4.3

### 3. Unicode Width Correctness Proven
- [ ] Corpus tests cover ZWJ sequences
- [ ] Corpus tests cover emoji
- [ ] Corpus tests cover combining marks
- [ ] No off-by-one wrapping errors
- [ ] Truncation at correct grapheme boundaries

**Verified by:** bd-2gx Gate 3, bd-16k, bd-2uk

### 4. Style System is Deterministic and Documented
- [ ] Explicit masks documented
- [ ] Inheritance rules documented
- [ ] Style merge is deterministic (same input = same output)
- [ ] Theme resolution documented
- [ ] No "heisenbug" style flickers

**Verified by:** bd-10x, bd-22q

### 5. Runtime Supports Required Features
- [ ] update/view loop works
- [ ] Ticks scheduling works
- [ ] Batch commands work
- [ ] Sequence commands work
- [ ] Deterministic simulator works
- [ ] Snapshot tests work

**Verified by:** bd-10i.8.2, bd-10i.8.3

### 6. Core Widgets for Harness UIs Exist and Tested
- [ ] Viewport/log viewer: bd-29v
- [ ] Status line / panel: bd-2dc
- [ ] Text input / prompt line: bd-gpe
- [ ] Progress: bd-35p
- [ ] Spinner: bd-35p
- [ ] All widgets have snapshot tests

**Verified by:** bd-10i.7 (Phase 5)

### 7. Documentation Complete
- [ ] "Build an agent harness UI" tutorial: bd-wo2
- [ ] Inline vs alt-screen explanation: bd-1u5
- [ ] IO ownership and one-writer rule guidance: bd-10i.12.6
- [ ] Windows limitations documented: bd-2ss
- [ ] API documentation (rustdoc) complete

**Verified by:** bd-10i.12 (Docs epic)

### 8. Quality Gates Pass (All Four)
- [ ] Gate 1: Inline mode stability
- [ ] Gate 2: Diff/presenter correctness
- [ ] Gate 3: Unicode width correctness
- [ ] Gate 4: Terminal cleanup

**Verified by:** bd-2gx

### 9. Performance Budgets Met
- [ ] Present (80×24, 5% changed): p50 < 1.0ms, p99 < 3.0ms
- [ ] Present (120×40, 5% changed): p50 < 2.0ms, p99 < 6.0ms
- [ ] Cell comparison: < 1ns
- [ ] Input parse: < 100µs/event

**Verified by:** bd-19x (benchmarks)

### 10. CI/CD Complete
- [ ] All tests pass on Linux, macOS, Windows
- [ ] Coverage meets thresholds
- [ ] Benchmarks run and tracked
- [ ] Release workflow configured
- [ ] Dependabot configured

**Verified by:** bd-xn2

## Ship Process
1. Create v1 tracking issue listing all criteria
2. Verify all beads above are closed
3. Run full test suite on all platforms
4. Update CHANGELOG.md with v1 release notes
5. Tag v1.0.0
6. Publish to crates.io

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 10 sections verified green
- [ ] No P0 or P1 issues open
- [ ] v1.0.0 tagged and published","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:19:32.880914588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:05:41.935183595Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:05:41.935112361Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tb84","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tb84","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tb84","depends_on_id":"bd-19x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tb84","depends_on_id":"bd-2gx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tb84","depends_on_id":"bd-gpe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tb84","depends_on_id":"bd-xn2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":556,"issue_id":"bd-tb84","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## V1 Ship Readiness Verification (2026-02-02)

**All 6 blocking dependencies CLOSED:** bd-2gx, bd-10i.12, bd-gpe, bd-10i.11.2, bd-19x, bd-xn2

**1. Inline Mode Stability:** VERIFIED
- 31/31 E2E PTY tests pass (inline_basic, inline_resize, inline_cursor_contract, inline_log_scroll, inline_many_logs, inline_custom_height, inline_ui_chrome)
- Cleanup tests pass (normal exit, SIGTERM, altscreen exit, mouse/paste/cursor restore)
- Unicode rendering stable (ASCII, accented, CJK, emoji, mixed)

**2. Diff/Presenter Correctness:** VERIFIED
- ftui-render unit tests pass (buffer, diff, presenter, sanitizer)
- Property test regression files tracked (cell.txt, presenter.txt, sanitize.txt)
- E2E ANSI tests pass (SGR colors, SGR reset, cursor position, sync output)

**3. Unicode Width Correctness:** VERIFIED
